source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/MixtureAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D6 STRUCTURAL oracle (D5 seeded-scalar assertions removed; see below). MixtureAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a 2-component Normal MixtureModel, then assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the 5-point exceedance grid. curve_length == 5 is the deterministic invariant, reproduced exactly across C++/R/Python AND the real-RMC.BestFit emitter (tools/oracle_emitter mirroring mixture_analysis::run(): serial seeded fit, CreateFrequencyAnalysisResults). D6 REALITY: the emitter does NOT reproduce the D5 self-computed seeded scalars (mixture weight, frequency curve, credible band, AIC/BIC/DIC). Unlike the sibling CompetingRisk/PointProcess analyses (which DO reproduce and are pinned exact), the standard-init MAP search over this strongly bimodal Normal-mixture likelihood lands on a different mode in the real C# vs the ported C++ core -- the mixture LogSumExp likelihood is float-reassociation sensitive, so a single last-bit difference flips the DE optimizer's basin (parameter[0] weight 0.50 vs 0.61; mode_curve 3917 vs 25909). This matches the D6 brief's design statement that the analyses are STRUCTURAL oracles only -- no numeric MCMC oracle exists upstream. thinning_interval pinned to 1; short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Parameter layout [w1, w2, mu1, sigma1, mu2, sigma2].
